pages.text: Vanilla tools work as they always have. What waits one tier ahead is $(italic)Tinkers' Construct$() — the chosen tool-tiering system in Novus, generally a day-three or later concern.
pages.title: Tools & Hearth

pages.text: $(bold)Tinkers' tools, in brief:$()$(br)$(li)Build a Part Builder + Tool Forge first (eventually a Smeltery)$(li)Tools made from Tinkers parts can be repaired and modified — they need not be thrown away when worn$(li)Six in-game Tinkers' books cover the rest — Materials and You, Puny Smelting, Mighty Smelting, Tinkers' Gadgetry, Encyclopedia of Tinkering, Fantastic Foundry
$(bold)Hunger & saturation:$()$(br2)$(italic)AppleSkin$() shows exactly how much hunger and saturation each food restores. Eat well-cooked foods and you'll rarely see hunger drop below half.
pages.title: AppleSkin

pages.text: $(bold)The hearth starts at the cooking pot.$() $(italic)Farmer's Delight's$() Cooking Pot is the earliest step into the food chain. Lit campfires count as heat sources, so you don't need iron tools to begin. See $(l:2_hearth_season/wild_edibles_hearth)Wild Edibles & Hearth$() for the full stove/pot/skillet setup.
pages.title: Cooking Pot
